lines.en_lit: itself is called “Shabbat,” as it is written: you shall rest on your Shabbat” Granted, Rav Pappa did not say as Rav Aḥa bar Ya’akov a verse that is written about the matter itself is preferable But what is the reason Rav Aḥa bar Ya’akov did not state in accordance with the opinion of Rav Pappa?

He requires this for that which was taught “And you shall afflict your souls on the ninth day of the month might one should start to afflict oneself on the ninth the verse states “at evening.” If “at evening,” might when darkness falls; the verse states “on the ninth,” How One begins to fast while it is still daytime; from here that one extends from non-sacred to sacred

I have only at the beginning From where at the conclusion of The verse states: “From evening until evening” And I only Yom Kippur; from where Festivals? The verse states: “You shall rest” I have only Festivals; from where Shabbatot? The verse states: “Your Shabbat” How so? Every place the term: Rest [shevut] is stated, from here that one appends from non-sacred to sacred.

And the tanna same same what does he do with “On the ninth day of the month”? He requires it, in accordance with which Ḥiyya bar Rav of Difti taught. As Ḥiyya bar Rav of Difti taught: “And you shall afflict your souls on the ninth But does one afflict oneself on the ninth Doesn’t one afflict oneself on the tenth Rather, to tell you: Anyone who eats and drinks on the ninth the verse ascribes him as though he fasted on ninth and the tenth.
ate food that is fit for eating, Rava said: one chews pepper on Yom Kippur, he is exempt, one chews ginger [zangvila] on Yom Kippur, he is exempt.

raises an objection Rabbi Meir would say From the implication of what is stated: “Then you shall count the fruit of it as forbidden,” do I not know that “trees for food,” Rather, what the verse states “trees for food”? a tree whose wood and fruit taste the same, must say this pepper to teach you that pepper is subject to of orla. And Eretz Yisrael lacks nothing, as it is stated “You will not lack anything in it”
This not difficult. This to moist; and that dry

Ravina said to Mareimar: But didn’t Rav Naḥman say it is permitted this cooked ginger [himalta] that comes from India, And we recite the blessing: Who creates the fruit of the ground, over it. This not difficult: This to wet and that to dry

The Sages taught one ate leaves of reeds he is exempt, grapevine shoots he is liable. What are these grapevine shoots? Rabbi Yitzḥak from Migdal said: All that sprouted between Rosh HaShana and Yom Kippur And Rav Kahana said: thirty days It was taught in accordance with Rabbi Yitzḥak from Migdal: one ate leaves of reeds he is exempt, but grapevine shoots he is liable. What are these grapevine shoots? all those that sprouted between Rosh HaShana and Yom Kippur.
drank fish brine or liquid he is exempt. if one drank vinegar, he is liable. Who is the mishna? It is Rabbi as it was taught Rabbi says: Vinegar revives the spirit

Rav Giddel bar Menashe from Birei DeNeresh taught halakha is not in accordance with Rabbi The year everyone went out and mixed and drank vinegar Rav Giddel heard and became angry He said: Say that I said only after the fact; did I say ab initio? Say that I said a little, did I say a lot? Say that I said pure but did I say diluted
